{ "report": "1 1. `Stored XSS` in legacy Message Stream notifications Files: - [notification/macros.vm](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/xwiki-platform_codex/xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-web/xwiki-platform-web-templates/src/main/resources/templates/notification/macros.vm:206) - [notification/email/macros.vm](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/xwiki-platform_codex/xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-web/xwiki-platform-web-templates/src/main/resources/templates/notification/email/macros.vm:319) - [DefaultMessageStream.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/xwiki-platform_codex/xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-legacy/xwiki-platform-legacy-messagestream/xwiki-platform-legacy-messagestream-api/src/main/java/org/xwiki/messagestream/internal/DefaultMessageStream.java:268) - [MessageSenderMacro.xml](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/xwiki-platform_codex/xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-legacy/xwiki-platform-legacy-messagestream/xwiki-platform-legacy-messagestream-ui/src/main/resources/Main/MessageSenderMacro.xml:680) Why this is vulnerable: - User input from `request.messagestream_message` is accepted and posted through `services.messageStream.post*Message(...)`. - `DefaultMessageStream.createMessageEvent()` stores that message directly into `Event.body` with no sanitization or escaping. - Notification templates later render `$messageEvent.body` directly inside `
` in both web notifications and HTML email notifications. - There is no `xml`/HTML escaping and no sanitizer on this field at render time. Exploit path: - Authenticated user posts a message such as ``. - The payload is stored as event body. - Any user who views the corresponding notification, or receives/render the HTML email notification, executes the payload. Impact: - Stored XSS. - Affects notification consumers, not only the attacker. - Can likely execute in the victim’s authenticated session in the web notification UI.", "response": "{ "judgement": "TP", "analysis": "The report is a true positive. The source is correctly identified: `MessageSenderMacro.xml` reads `request.messagestream_message`, trims it, and for authenticated users passes it unchanged to `services.messageStream.postPublicMessage(...)`, `postPersonalMessage(...)`, `postMessageToGroup(...)`, or `postDirectMessageToUser(...)`. In `DefaultMessageStream.createMessageEvent()`, the only processing is `StringUtils.left(message, 2000)`; the body is stored with `e.setBody(...)` and there is no HTML escaping or sanitization. The sink is also correctly identified and is reachable: the legacy message-stream notification templates for `publicMessage`, `personalMessage`, `directMessage`, and `groupMessage` call macros that output `$messageEvent.body` directly inside `
` in both the web notification template and the HTML email template. This is not a shallow or theoretically unreachable path: these event types have concrete templates and the notification renderer executes them for matching events. No sanitizer is applied on this path. XWiki's template engine also does not auto-escape this output here: `TemplateManager.execute()` runs the Velocity template, and `InternalTemplateManager` / `TemplateAsyncRenderer` wrap the evaluated result as a `RawBlock` in the target syntax; the notification UI then renders that block to `html/5.0`. So attacker-controlled HTML is preserved as HTML, not converted to safe text. This matches CWE-079. In real use, this is authenticated stored XSS in the web notification UI. The HTML email sink is more client-dependent, but the web sink alone is sufficient to classify the issue as a real security vulnerability.", "FP_Reason": [], "evidence": "Data flow: `request.messagestream_message` -> `MessageSenderMacro.xml` posts `$message` through `services.messageStream.post*Message(...)` -> `DefaultMessageStream.createMessageEvent()` stores it with `e.setBody(StringUtils.left(message, 2000))` -> legacy notification templates render `$messageEvent.body` directly.\n\nRelevant code paths:\n1. Message submission: `Main/MessageSenderMacro.xml` sets `$message = \"$!{request.messagestream_message.trim()}\"` and then calls `$services.messageStream.postPublicMessage($message)`, `postPersonalMessage($message)`, `postMessageToGroup($message, ...)`, or `postDirectMessageToUser($message, ...)`, with only guest / CSRF / empty-message checks.\n2. Storage: `DefaultMessageStream.java` `createMessageEvent()` contains `e.setBody(StringUtils.left(message, 2000));` and does not sanitize.\n3. Web notification sink: `templates/notification/publicMessage.vm` calls `#displayMessageNotification(...)`; `templates/notification/macros.vm` macro `displayMessageNotification` contains `
$messageEvent.body
`. `groupMessage` uses the same pattern.\n4. HTML email sink: `templates/notification/email/publicMessage.html.vm` calls `#displayMessageNotificationRightCell(...)`; `templates/notification/email/macros.vm` macro `displayMessageNotificationRightCell` contains `
$messageEvent.body
`.\n5. Renderer behavior: `DefaultNotificationDisplayer.renderNotification()` calls `templateManager.execute(template)`. `InternalTemplateManager.getXDOM()` / `TemplateAsyncRenderer.renderVelocity()` wrap evaluated Velocity output in `RawBlock(result, targetSyntax)`. `InternalNotificationsRenderer.render()` then renders the notification block with `BlockRenderer` `html/5.0`. This preserves raw HTML output rather than escaping it.\n6. Supporting framework evidence: XWiki rendering tests such as `DefaultWikiMacroTest` expect raw `html/5.0` content to stay as `onRawText [] [html/5.0]`, which is consistent with raw HTML preservation.\n\nReal-world trigger: an authenticated user can post ``. That payload is stored in the event body and later inserted into the notification HTML blockquote shown to recipients in the web notification UI, enabling stored XSS.", "new_reason:": "" }" }

{
  "report": "2
2. `Likely XSS / unsafe URL injection` in extension metadata links
File:
- [extension.vm](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/xwiki-platform_codex/xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-web/xwiki-platform-web-templates/src/main/resources/templates/extension.vm:448)
Relevant lines:
- `href="$extension.webSite"`
- `href="$extension.repository.descriptor.URI"`
- `href="$extension.scm.url"`
- `href="$extension.scm.connection.path"`
- `href="$extension.issueManagement.getURL()"`
Why this is risky:
- These values are inserted into `href` attributes without a URL sanitizer.
- Visible label text is escaped, but the link target is not validated against dangerous schemes.
- If extension metadata can be controlled by a malicious extension package or repository entry, values like `javascript:alert(1)` or crafted URLs would become clickable active content.
Confidence:
- High that this is unsafe coding.
- Slightly lower than finding 1 because exploitability depends on whether attacker-controlled extension metadata can reach this view in the target deployment model.
- Still worth treating as a real security issue because XWiki imports/displays external extension metadata and the template performs no scheme validation.",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "TP",
  "analysis": "The report is a true positive. In `extension.vm`, XWiki renders extension metadata directly into active `href` attributes (`$extension.webSite`, `$extension.scm.url`, `$extension.issueManagement.getURL()`, etc.) without URL validation and without even XML-escaping the attribute value. The source is real: `AbstractExtensionRESTResource.createExtension()` copies `PROP_EXTENSION_WEBSITE`, `PROP_EXTENSION_SCMURL`, and `PROP_EXTENSION_ISSUEMANAGEMENT_URL` from the extension store into the extension object as raw strings, and `ExtensionIndexStore.add()` indexes `extension.getWebSite()` unchanged. This is not shallow or unreachable reasoning: `ExtensionIT.testShowDetails()` builds a remote `TestExtension`, sets website/SCM/issue-management metadata, adds it to the repository, opens the Extension Manager UI, and asserts that the rendered `href` equals the original metadata value. XWiki also has an intended defense path that is skipped here: `macros.vm` defines `#getSanitizedURLAttributeValue()` using `services.security.url.parseToSafeURI()` and `services.html.isAttributeSafe()`, and `MacrosTest` shows `javascript:alert('foobar')` is rejected by that safe path. Therefore the source/sink pair is correct, the path is reachable, no sanitizer blocks it, and the finding matches CWE-079 as a click-triggered stored XSS / unsafe URL injection. Exploitation requires attacker-controlled extension metadata and a victim clicking the rendered link, so the issue is not an automatic page-load XSS, but it is still a real vulnerability. `repository.descriptor.URI` and `scm.connection.path` are somewhat less certain because their controllability depends on repository / SCM parsing semantics, but the report as a whole remains true because multiple clearly attacker-controlled string fields reach the sink unsanitized.",
  "FP_Reason": [],
  "evidence": "Sink: `xwiki-platform-web-templates/src/main/resources/templates/extension.vm:448-470` renders `<a href=\"$extension.webSite\">`, `<a href=\"$extension.repository.descriptor.URI\">`, `<a href=\"$extension.scm.url\">`, `<a href=\"$extension.scm.connection.path\">`, and `<a href=\"$extension.issueManagement.getURL()\">`. Source population: `xwiki-platform-repository-server-api/.../AbstractExtensionRESTResource.java:349-387` does `extension.setWebsite((String) ...)`, `scm.setUrl((String) ...)`, and `issueManagement.setUrl((String) ...)` from stored metadata with no scheme check. Index/storage path: `xwiki-platform-extension-index/.../ExtensionIndexStore.java:588-593` stores `extension.getWebSite()` unchanged. Reachability proof: `xwiki-platform-extension-test-tests/.../ExtensionIT.java:355-391` sets `extension.setWebsite(\"http://www.alice.com\")`, `extension.setScm(...)`, `extension.setIssueManagement(...)`, then asserts `webSiteLink.getAttribute(\"href\") == extension.getWebSite() + '/'`, proving metadata -> rendered link end to end. Existing sanitizer not used: `xwiki-platform-web-templates/.../macros.vm:3234-3244` defines `#getSanitizedURLAttributeValue()` with `services.security.url.parseToSafeURI()` + `services.html.isAttributeSafe()`, and `xwiki-platform-web-templates/.../MacrosTest.java:347-385` verifies `javascript:alert('foobar')` is rejected. Same-template contrast: `extension.vm:1022-1031` checks supporter URLs with `services.html.isAttributeSafe('a', 'href', $extensionSupporterPlan.uRL)` before rendering, showing these metadata links are missing an available protection.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}"
}
